ogTitle: Nyrang Creek vs O'Connell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Nyrang Creek and O'Connell.
heading: Nyrang Creek vs O'Connell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Nyrang Creek, New South Wales and O'Connell, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: O'Connell has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Nyrang Creek (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Nyrang Creek or O'Connell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Nyrang Creek has a flood risk score of 57/100, while O'Connell scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Nyrang Creek scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. O'Connell scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Nyrang Creek or O'Connell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. O'Connell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
